Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

Thanks! That's look great.
Did you have the chance to create keytab using SHA2 and mount the lustre FS with it?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Thanks! That's look great.
> Did you have the chance to create keytab using SHA2 and mount the lustre FS with it?

Indeed, I upgraded my local test system to CentOS Stream 9 recently, where I also run my KDC. With this distro older enc types are no longer supported, so gss context negotiation is done using AES256_CTS_HMAC_SHA384_192.
Unfortunately I think it would be very complicated to have that in Maloo right now. It uses a single KDC, and it needs to support a bunch of different distros.

parents.subject: LU-17190 osc: account DIO in flight using server-side locking

commit.subject: LU-18256 gss: support SHA2 enctypes
commit.message:
LU-18256 gss: support SHA2 enctypes

Introduce support for ENCTYPE_AES128_CTS_HMAC_SHA256_128 and
ENCTYPE_AES256_CTS_HMAC_SHA384_192 encryption types that are used by
GSS code for authentication context.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-1
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-2
Test-Parameters: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I42ab758b42b24c64647cd771887a2fd26bc55394
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56489
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Aurelien Degremont <adegremont@nvidia.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
